_deprecated: This file is NOT the Claude Code plugin manifest. The real plugin manifest is at .claude-plugin/plugin.json (per https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces). This file is kept as extended metadata documenting envSchema, requiredMcpServers, quality gates, etc. — fields that don't fit the official plugin.json spec. Consumed by bootstrap/install.ts for the post-install env-setup wizard. May be renamed to vc-qa-metadata.json or moved to docs/ in a future commit.
name: vc-qa
description: Agentic QA for Virto Commerce — covers storefront + Admin SPA across multiple customer environments. Ships 14 agents, 20 skills, 16 commands, 19 knowledge files, 99 regression suites (universal + reference + vcst-specific subsets). Methodology + agent crew + authoring framework — customers run the universal subset and author their own for custom features.
author: Virto Commerce

inScope: Built-in modules: catalog, customer, pricing, inventory, marketing, notifications, cms, orders, store, search, shipping, returns, loyalty, seo, assets, channels, contracts, image-tools, import-export, push-messages, whitelabeling

requiredMcpServers.name: playwright-edge
requiredMcpServers.purpose: Admin SPA testing + fallback for storefront

optionalMcpServers.name: atlassian
optionalMcpServers.purpose: JIRA bug filing, sprint planning, ticket triage

optionalMcpServers.name: claude_ai_VirtoOZ_for_virtocommerce_com_docs
optionalMcpServers.purpose: Primary VC documentation source (12 topic-scoped tools)

_meta.description: Required + optional env vars the customer provides at install time. Validated by bootstrap/install.ts. Three buckets per docs/configuration.md: plugin-supplied (in .env.defaults, this repo), customer-required (in .env.${TEST_ENV}, customer-edited), customer-secret (in .env.local, gitignored).

TEST_ENV.description: Active environment name. Selects .env.${TEST_ENV}. Arbitrary string (dev, qa, staging, prod, customer_eu, anything) — kebab-case rejected because the suffix-promotion in .env.local requires [a-z0-9_]+.

ENV_RISK.description: Risk class of this env (NOT env name). 'production' refuses admin-write suites by default. Each env profile sets its own.

STOREFRONT_PROFILE.description: Storefront flavor. Orchestrator skips Frontend suites whose storefrontProfile[] doesn't include this.

MODULES_ENABLED.description: Comma-separated list of installed VC modules (catalog, customer, marketing, cms, loyalty, etc.). Orchestrator skips Backend suites whose requiresModules[] aren't in this set. Empty = no filter.

JIRA_PROJECT_KEY.description: Customer's JIRA project key. Used by /qa-bug, /qa-status, /qa-test-plan, /qa-defect.

FRONT_URL.description: Customer's VC storefront base URL (e.g. https://shop.example.com).

BACK_URL.description: Customer's VC platform / Admin SPA base URL (e.g. https://admin.example.com).

STORE_ID.description: Customer's primary store ID in their VC deployment.

USER_PASSWORD.description: Storefront test user password. Supports per-env suffix promotion (USER_PASSWORD_QA, USER_PASSWORD_STAGING, etc.).

ADMIN_PASSWORD.description: Admin SPA admin password. Supports per-env suffix promotion.

_meta.description: Default quality gates. Per docs/versioning.md these are Tier A and won't change without a major version bump.
